CAN: verify det map {-1,+1} identifies two O(3) components via pytorch
prove coset structure: ref×ref ∈ SO(3), rot×ref ∈ O(3)\SO(3)
exclude O(3)\SO(3) elements from SO(3) via z3 UNSAT
verify O(3)/SO(3) ≅ Z_2 via sympy determinant algebra
identify Pin(3)/Spin(3) = Z_2 covers the O(3)/SO(3) split via Clifford
use e3nn parity (p=±1) to distinguish O(3) vs SO(3) irreps
encode O(3)→SO(3) edge in rustworkx tower DAG
CANNOT: add complex structure at this level (use SO(3)↔U(3) coupling)
require det=1 for complex matrices (use U(3)↔SU(3) coupling)

pytorch.reason: load-bearing: pytorch det map det:O(3)→{-1,+1} identifies two components; product of two O(3)\SO(3) elements is in SO(3) (coset closure).

pyg.reason: not used in this pairwise O(3)↔SO(3) coupling probe; other cross-tool coupling deferred per four-sim-kinds doctrine.

z3.reason: load-bearing: z3 UNSAT proves det=-1 is incompatible with SO membership; the orientation constraint strictly separates O(3) into two components.

cvc5.reason: not used in this pairwise O(3)↔SO(3) coupling probe; other cross-tool coupling deferred per four-sim-kinds doctrine.

sympy.reason: load-bearing: sympy verifies that O(3)/SO(3) ≅ Z_2 by showing the det map has image {-1,+1} and that SO(3) is a normal subgroup.

clifford.reason: load-bearing: Pin(3) ⊂ Cl(3,0) double covers O(3); Spin(3) = Pin(3)^+ (even subalgebra) double covers SO(3); the O(3)/SO(3) split corresponds to Pin(3)/Spin(3) = Z_2.

geomstats.reason: load-bearing: geomstats SO(3) manifold is connected; O(3) has two connected components; the coupling exposes this topological difference.

e3nn.reason: load-bearing: e3nn differentiates even/odd parity: D^l with p=+1 is SO(3) irrep, p=-1 is O(3) irrep including reflection; O(3)/SO(3) coupling controls which parity is accessible.

rustworkx.reason: load-bearing: rustworkx encodes O(3)→SO(3) as a directed edge; verified via adjacency and path length between the two nodes.

xgi.reason: not used in this pairwise O(3)↔SO(3) coupling probe; other cross-tool coupling deferred per four-sim-kinds doctrine.

toponetx.reason: not used in this pairwise O(3)↔SO(3) coupling probe; other cross-tool coupling deferred per four-sim-kinds doctrine.

gudhi.reason: not used in this pairwise O(3)↔SO(3) coupling probe; other cross-tool coupling deferred per four-sim-kinds doctrine.

two_reflections_compose_to_SO3.detail: Product of two reflections has det=+1: in SO(3) (coset product rule)

rotation_times_reflection_is_O3_minus_SO.detail: Rotation × reflection has det=-1: stays in O(3)\SO(3)

SO3_is_subgroup_of_O3.detail: SO(3) rotation has det=+1: SO(3) ⊂ O(3) confirmed

z3_O_minus_SO_excluded.detail: z3 UNSAT: det=-1 and det=+1 cannot both hold; O\SO excluded from SO

z3_O_minus_SO_exists.detail: z3 SAT: O(1) has a det=-1 element (x=-1); SO(1) constraint excludes it

sympy_coset_Z2.detail: O/SO coset structure: det(-1)*det(-1) = +1 (Z_2 group law)

sympy_so3_normal_in_o3.detail: det(R * Ref * R^{-1}) = -1: O(3)\SO(3) is preserved under SO(3) conjugation

clifford_spin3_even_covers_SO3.detail: Spin(3) rotor is in even subalgebra of Cl(3,0): covers SO(3)

clifford_pin3_odd_covers_O3_minus_SO3.detail: Pin(3) odd versors (grade-1) double cover O(3)\SO(3) reflections

e3nn_parity_distinguishes_O3_SO3.detail: e3nn: p=+1 and p=-1 are distinct irreps; O(3)/SO(3) coupling controls parity access

geomstats_so3_connected.detail: geomstats SO(3): identity belongs; SO(3) is the connected component of O(3)

rustworkx_O3_SO3_edge.detail: rustworkx: O(3)→SO(3) directed edge exists (orientation ratchet step)

rustworkx_O3_before_SO3.detail: Topological sort: O(3) precedes SO(3) (O(3) is less constrained)

coset_product_in_O3_minus_SO3.detail: Reflection × Rotation stays in O(3)\SO(3): det=-1

SO3_in_O3_exact.detail: SO(3) rotation is in both SO(3) and O(3): coupling is exact on SO(3)
